arts.ttl: Wolves 'tried everything' against Nottingham Forest - Pereira
arts.sum: Vítor Pereira says he is "proud" of his Wolves side because they "tried everything" against Nottingham Forest in their 3-0 home defeat at Molineux.

Wolves had failed to take their chances before the break and struggled to create many after it, and Taiwo Awoniyi wrapped up the win for the visitors in second half injury time, subjecting Vitor Pereira to a first loss in the Molineux dugout. First, Hee Chan Hwang picked up a loose ball in the Forest half, drove toward goal, and he cut back to find Strand Larsen in the six-yard box, but his effort was blocked on the line by Murillo.

arts.ttl: Forest beat Wolves for sixth straight Prem win
arts.sum: Chris Wood scored his 12th Premier League goal of the season as third-placed Nottingham Forest beat Wolverhampton Wanderers 3-0 at Molineux Stadium on Monday, keeping them on course for an unlikely title challenge.

arts.ttl: Premier League release statement on why VAR decided not to award Nottingham Forest a penalty vs Wolves
Nottingham Forest extend winning run as Gibbs-White returns to haunt Wolves
arts.sum: Just as Wolves had started to dream of a brighter future under Vítor Pereira, it was the ghosts of the past that haunted Molineux as their former head coach Nuno Espírito Santo and academy product Morgan Gibbs-White snatched the points to send Nottingham Forest level with second-placed Arsenal. Wolves were unbeaten under Pereira and the superior side for much of the match but as they saw chances come and go, while portraying Gibbs-White as a pantomime villain, it seemed inevitable what the outcome would be. Nuno took Wolves into Europe and is plotting the same trick with Forest after a sixth victory in a row, sealed by goals from Gibbs-White, Chris Wood and Taiwo Awoniyi. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Wolverhampton Wanderers 0-3 Nottingham Forest: Highlights, man of the match, stats as Nuno Espirito Santo defeats former team
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest extend Premier League winning run with 3-0 victory over wasteful Wolverhampton Wanderers, marking their first six successive top-flight wins since 1979.

arts.ttl: Nottingham Forest move within six points of leaders Liverpool with win at Wolves
arts.sum: A brilliant season under Nuno Espirito Santo continued at Molineux thanks to goals from Morgan Gibbs-White, Chris Wood and Taiwo Awoniyi.

arts.ttl: ‘A revelation’: Jamie Carragher hails Steve Cooper’s ‘brilliant buy’ as Nottingham Forest beat Wolves
Wolves v Nottingham Forest: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Minute-by-minute coverage of the 8pm UK time kick-off Any comments? You can send Michael an email If Forest keep this form up, it’s not inconceivable that Hudson-Odoi could add to his three England caps. That left wing spot is definitely up for grabs. Anthony Gordon probably is the pick at the moment on form, with Phil Foden, Jadon Sancho, Jamie Gittens, Eberechi Eze, Jack Grealish also in the reckoning. Am I forgetting anyone? I guess Bukayo Saka or Jarrod Bowen could play there, but not their natural position. England manager Thomas Tuchel is also in the house. The only English footballers in either line up are Tommy Doyle (Wolves), Elliot Anderson, Callum Hudson-Odoi and Morgan Gibbs-White (Forest). Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Wolves vs. Nottingham Forest team news: Shock PL debut for home side
arts.sum: Wolverhampton Wanderers and Nottingham Forest announce their first XIs for Monday's Premier League game at Molineux.
